communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All adverse medical event records filed with the Washington Department of Health between Jan. 1, 2023 and Jan. 1, 2025.

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Nolan

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) for Gerald Gallop.
Request copy of history of assignments NAVPERS form 1070/605, Legion of Merit award citations, Meritorious Service medal citation, Navy Commendation medal citations, navy achievement medal citations, Air Medal award citations, all award citations, Cumulative Record of Training (NAVPERS Form 5300/16), Transfers and Receipts NAVPERS 601-12 from 1970 to 2000, Administrative remarks NAVPERS 1070/613, all dates of promotions.
Please redact any information per Freedom of Information Act, U.S.C. § 552(b)(3) and U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).

VETERAN INFORMATION
Name of veteran: Gerald S. Gallop
Branch of service: United States Navy
Rank: CAPT
Service: 1980 to 2000
http://1980.usnaclasses.net/wp-content/uploads/sites/27/2021/02/1980-Graduation-and-Commissioning-Ceremonies-28-May-1980.pdf

pilot tours flying F-4 Phantom, F-14 Tomcat, and F/A-18 Hornet.  TOPGUN

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records concerning below:
Name: Henry Stringer
Branch: United States Army
DOB: August 27, 1949
Approximate Dates of Service: 1968 to 1989

Please provide the following military service information:
1. dates of service, rank, stations, list of awards, unit decorations
2. Section VII CURRENT AND PREVIOUS ASSIGNMENTS form
3. hard copy of Bronze Star Medal award citation
4. Military Education/ training courses form 
5. Section V MISCELLANEOUS Form
6. request hard copy of Army commendation award citations
7. Section II Classification And Assignment Data

Historical research Military History - Vietnam War.

First name: Henry
Last name: Stringer
DOB: Aug 27, 1949

I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information. Redact per U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).
Please mail hardcopies to:

MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley
